wiki.content: "back to friends" is a song by singer-songwriter sombr. Written and produced solely by Sombr, the indie-pop song was released on December 27, 2024 and served as the lead single to Sombr's debut album, I Barely Know Her (2025). The music video, directed by Gus Black, was released on April 3, 2025, and features model Charlotte D'Alessio. It won Best Alternative Video at the 2025 MTV Video Music Awards. Lyrically, the song captures the melodrama of teenage love. Back To Friends is a hauntingly introspective song about the emotional aftermath of an intimate connection that has been blurred by shifting boundaries. The lyrics explore the complex tension between physical intimacy and the struggle to return to a platonic friendship afterward. The song captures the vulnerability and confusion of trying to reconcile the deeper feelings that were once shared with the reality of emotional detachment. The song became Sombr's breakout hit, alongside his 2025 single "undressed", both of which entered several charts concurrently after going viral on the video-sharing app TikTok. "Back to Friends" experienced a significant surge in on-demand streams in March 2025 after going viral on the video-sharing platform TikTok.[2] The song subsequently debuted at number 140 on the Billboard Global 200 for the chart dated April 5, 2025, marking Sombr's first entry on the chart.[8] In its tenth week, it entered the top ten at number nine,[9] and later peaked at number five on the chart dated September 27, 2025.[10] In the United States, back to friends debuted at #8 on the Bubbling Under Hot 100 chart dated April 5, 2025. The following week, the song entered the Billboard Hot 100 at #90, becoming Sombr's first appearance on the chart. In its ninth week, it jumped 26 spots from #62 to enter the top 40 at #36. In its 40th week, it reached the top 10 at it's peak of #7 staying for 2 non-consecutive weeks in January 2026, becoming his highest-charting single and first top-10 entry on the chart. back to friends also marked Sombr's first appearance on several rock and alternative charts, including topping the Hot Rock & Alternative Songs for 16 weeks as of February 21, 2026. In addition to rising streams, the song gained increased radio airplay, topping the Alternative Airplay & Pop Airplay charts, and reaching #2 on the Rock & Alternative Airplay chart. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.
